records.ety: [OE. agens, ageynes, AS. ongegn. The s is adverbial, orig. a genitive ending. See Again.]

defs: Abreast; opposite to; facing; towards; as, against the mouth of a river; -- in this sense often preceded by over.
quotes: Jacob saw the angels of God come against him.

defs: From an opposite direction so as to strike or come in contact with; in contact with; upon; as, hail beats against the roof.

In opposition to, whether the opposition is of sentiment or of action; on the other side; counter to; in contrariety to; hence, adverse to; as, against reason; against law; to run a race against time.
quotes: The gate would have been shut against her.
An argument against the use of steam.

defs: By of before the time that; in preparation for; so as to be ready for the time when.
quotes: Urijah the priest made it, against King Ahaz came from Damascus.
